news.title: Arkade Developers' net profit declines 96.63% to ₹5.29 crore in FY26
news.para: Arkade Developers saw its net profit plummet by 96.63% in the financial year 2025-26. Profit after tax fell to ₹5.29 crore from ₹156.92 crore in the previous year. However, total income rose by 19.23% to ₹828.16 crore. The company's pre-sales and collections also showed growth. Arkade Developers has a substantial project pipeline valued at ₹12,800 crore.

news.title: Smartworks to invest ₹70 crore to add 4.92 lakh sq ft office space in Bengaluru
news.para: Smartworks Coworking Spaces is expanding its presence in Bengaluru by leasing 4.92 lakh sq ft at Sattva Aura. This significant expansion will require an investment of approximately ₹70 crore. The company plans to fund this growth through internal accruals or issue proceeds. This move underscores Smartworks' continued expansion in the Indian coworking market.

news.title: Aroundtown's profit halves $138.4 million in Q1 2026
news.para: German landlord Aroundtown saw its first-quarter earnings dip significantly, primarily due to a lack of property revaluations compared to last year and increased finance costs. Despite a slight uptick in German commercial property prices, the company's funds from operations also declined. The full impact of Middle East warfare on the real estate market remains uncertain.

news.title: Maharashtra cabinet waives ₹637 crore stamp duty & registration fees for New Nagpur project
news.para: Maharashtra cabinet has approved a complete waiver on stamp duty and registration charges for land acquisition for the New Nagpur Business District and New Outer Ring Road projects. This move, expected to save ₹637 crore, aims to accelerate the implementation of these mega infrastructure developments in Nagpur district.

news.title: SC slams Rajasthan over failure to curb illegal sand mining
news.para: The Supreme Court has strongly criticized the Rajasthan government for its poor efforts to stop illegal sand mining in the Chambal region. The court noted a lack of effective surveillance and monitoring systems. This inaction has led to severe environmental damage and threatens wildlife. The court also expressed concern over mining near a national highway bridge.

news.title: Pune office leasing slows down in Jan-March this year; rents rise amid supply crunch
news.para: Pune's office space demand moderated in Q1 2026, with net absorption falling to 11 lakh sq ft. Despite a dip, Global Capability Centers continue to drive demand, accounting for 55% of the market. A sharp 69% drop in supply has tightened the market, pushing vacancy to 11.6% and rentals up by 6%.

news.title: Register rental agreements online, avoid legal hassles: Maharashtra's registration department
news.para: Property owners are urged to opt for registered leave-and-licence agreements over notarised ones. Registered pacts offer stronger legal backing and automatically share tenant data with police, streamlining verification. This clarification follows confusion in Pimpri-Chinchwad, where police issued notices for tenant details, despite existing online registration systems. Uniform guidelines are sought to prevent owner harassment.

news.title: Construction costs up 25% since global conflicts began, developers flag material shortages: CREDAI
news.para: Construction costs have surged over 25 percent due to global conflicts. Supply chain issues, labor shortages, and rising material prices are impacting builders. Executives note challenges in material availability, stressing project timelines. The organized sector is better equipped to handle these pressures. Developers are seeking RERA timeline relief. Technology adoption is being explored to mitigate labor shortages.

news.title: Prayagraj civic body to launch OTS scheme for house tax arrear in June 2026
news.para: Prayagraj Municipal Corporation is launching a One Time Settlement (OTS) scheme in early June, waiving interest on pending house tax dues for over 90,000 property owners. This four-month scheme aims to recover Rs 125 crore in outstanding taxes, including Rs 90 crore in waived interest, benefiting both residential and commercial defaulters.

news.title: Kolkata civic body sets May 27 deadline for illegal building notice hearings
news.para: Kolkata Municipal Corporation has issued a May 27 deadline for illegal building notices. Demolitions are happening in several areas. Residents of occupied buildings are protesting, claiming developers misled them. Many buildings in areas like Tiljala-Topsia are flagged as illegal. KMC officials are compiling lists for further action.

news.title: ED conducts searches in Punjab in real estate 'fraud' case
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has conducted searches across Punjab and Chandigarh targeting a real estate company and its promoters in a money laundering probe. The investigation is linked to alleged fraud against the Greater Mohali Area Development Authority (GMADA), involving substantial outstanding liabilities and diversion of project funds.

news.title: Grade-A office vacancy may decline 50 bps this fiscal: Crisil
news.para: Vacancy in India’s Grade A commercial office market is expected to decline by about 50 basis points to 15.5-16% by the end of the current fiscal, according to Crisil Ratings. The decline will be supported by net leasing growth, demand from flexible workspace operators and GCCs, and scheduled completion of ongoing projects, though global uncertainty and AI-led disruption remain key risks.

news.title: BPTP to invest ₹1,200 crore to develop a residential project in Gurugram
news.para: BPTP has launched Downtown 66, a residential project in Sector 66, Gurugram, with an estimated gross development value of about ₹2,500 crore. The project will comprise 504 residences in 3BHK and 3.5BHK configurations, with prices starting from ₹5.32 crore, and will be developed with an estimated investment of ₹1,200 crore.

news.title: Property tax sop for business spaces if free parking offered: Ahmedabad civic body
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation is offering property tax exemptions to commercial establishments that open their parking facilities to the public free of charge. This initiative aims to reduce roadside parking and ease city traffic congestion. Participating businesses must consent via a form, manage parking through the AmadaPark app, and install signage. Failure to comply will disqualify them from the tax benefit.

news.title: Residents push for Kirti Nagar-Palam metro extension till Dwarka Expressway
news.para: Residents of new Gurgaon and Dwarka sectors are demanding an extension of the Kirti Nagar-Palam Metro corridor to Dwarka and Dwarka Expressway due to increasing traffic and inadequate public transport. They seek better metro and bus connectivity to ease congestion on major roads and reduce reliance on private vehicles.

news.title: NGT bench forms panel to probe illegal sand mining in Balasore
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has formed a joint committee to investigate allegations of illegal sand mining on the Subarnarekha river in Odisha's Balasore district. The committee, comprising representatives from district administration, SEIAA, and OSPCB, will submit a report within four weeks. This action follows a petition by local residents concerned about environmental degradation and revenue loss.

news.title: Government adopts category-based land valuation in Taleigao
news.para: Goa's revenue department has replaced its uniform land valuation system with a category-based approach, piloting the change in Taleigao. This new methodology assigns different minimum land rates to plots within a village based on location and amenities, aiming to address inconsistencies and revenue loss from the previous uniform rate system.

news.title: Telangana government secures ₹13,600 crore refinancing from IRFC for Hyderabad Metro Rail
news.para: Hyderabad Metro Rail has secured a significant financial boost with a Rs 13,600 crore refinancing agreement from IRFC. This deal aims to alleviate the project's debt burden and support future expansion, reinforcing the state government's commitment to enhancing public transport in the rapidly growing city. The loan will be repaid over 20 years.

news.title: Spouse’s name on property papers not enough to tax sale proceeds: ITAT
news.para: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that adding a spouse's name to property for convenience doesn't automatically make them liable for entire sale proceeds. The tribunal directed tax officers to verify if the husband was the sole funder and owner, and if capital gains were already taxed in his return, offering relief to a Mumbai taxpayer.

news.title: Over 40 housing societies in Ghaziabad fail fire safety test
news.para: Ghaziabad building owners exceeding 15 meters in height or 500 sqm floor area without a fire NOC will now bear firefighting costs. Notices issued to 43 societies for lacking or non-functional safety equipment follow a recent fire. Officials found widespread violations, including blocked access, prompting a crackdown on non-compliance.
